title: Broccoli and Stilton soup
subtitle: Creamed broccoli with crumbled blue cheese stirred in at the end.
excerpt: Broccoli blended smooth with vegetable stock, then finished with crumbled Stilton stirred in off the heat. The cheese does not melt into a uniform colour but leaves pale streaks through the green. Four ingredients carry it.
sourceNotes: Broccoli and Stilton soup is a firmly British combination, drawing on the tradition of pairing strong blue cheese with brassica vegetables. Stilton (protected designation of origin since 1996) has long been used as a flavouring ingredient in British soups and sauces, particularly in the Midlands counties where it is produced. The soup became a restaurant and home-kitchen standard in the 1990s.

content.text: The key to this soup is restraint with the Stilton. The cheese goes in off the heat, crumbled rather than melted in over a flame, so it softens without breaking down into a uniform grey. You want pale veins of cheese running through the green.

This is classed as intermediate because the timing is precise: broccoli overcooks quickly into something grey and sulphurous. The fix is simple but needs attention: get the broccoli tender in the stock, then blend immediately and add the cheese off the heat.

items.name: Broccoli
items.prepNote: about 500 g, cut into florets, stalk peeled and chopped

content.text: A large saucepan and stick blender are the only kit. Use the broccoli stalk as well as the florets. It adds body and the flavour is the same.

Melt {{unsalted-butter}} of butter in a large saucepan over a medium heat. Add the onion and a pinch of salt. Sweat for 8 minutes until soft. Add {{garlic}} of sliced garlic and cook for 2 minutes more.

Add the broccoli florets and stalk pieces. Pour over {{stock-vegetable}} of hot vegetable stock. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook for 8 to 10 minutes, no more, until the broccoli is just tender. Do not let it cook beyond this point.

Remove from the heat immediately. Blend with a stick blender until smooth. Stir in the double cream if using. Crumble in {{stilton}} of Stilton and stir gently, do not return to the heat. Season with {{black-pepper}} of black pepper; the cheese supplies salt. Serve straight away.

items.fix: Nothing rescues already-grey broccoli soup, but the flavour is still good. For next time, time the broccoli carefully, 8 minutes at a simmer, then blend immediately.
items.cause: Broccoli overcooked, breaking down the chlorophyll.
items.symptom: Soup is grey-green rather than bright green

items.fix: Always add the Stilton off the heat. Gentle residual warmth softens it; direct heat makes it stringy.
items.cause: The soup was still on the heat when the Stilton was added.

items.fix: Use unsalted stock or dilute with hot water. Add the Stilton in smaller amounts next time, tasting as you go.
items.cause: Stilton is very salty. Stock cubes add more.

items.fix: Reduce the stock by 150 to 200 ml next time, or simmer the blended soup briefly to reduce it. Do not reduce it with the Stilton in.
items.cause: Too much stock relative to broccoli.

content.text: Broccoli and Stilton is a distinctly British pairing. Stilton's protected status ties it to three English counties, and the tradition of using it as a cooking ingredient rather than solely as a cheeseboard cheese is particular to British cookery. The soup appears across gastropubs, farm shops, and home kitchens throughout the Midlands and beyond. It is an autumn and winter soup: filling enough to stand as a light lunch, elegant enough for a first course.
